search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae telehealth chest pain guideline red flags UAE
site:nice.org.uk CG95 stable chest pain assessment characteristics investigations
candidate_sources_rejected: non-acute label used to exclude acute coronary syndrome
automatic angina diagnosis, risk score, ECG, imaging, medicine, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Current acuity and stability require direct assessment.
No diagnosis, test, treatment, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with recurrent or non-acute chest discomfort after clinician triage; acute symptoms, pregnancy, children, trauma, pulmonary embolism, aortic, gastrointestinal, and musculoskeletal contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact stable chest-pain section supports pain character, location, exertional relation, relieving factors, cardiovascular risk, prior investigations, and clinician-led assessment without confirming angina. The exact stable assessment section supports recording existing ECG, imaging, or test context and clinician-decided follow-up without automatically ordering investigations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag and referral section supports clinician-assessed acute or unstable features, abnormal observations, cardiorespiratory concern, and escalation documentation without asserting non-acute safety.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current onset, severity, exertional pattern, observations, ECG, cardiac risk, acute features, cause, diagnosis, investigation, medicine, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
